heading: Close
body:
Cursors consume memory and network resources both in the client
application and in the connected instance of MongoDB. Use
[close()](https://mongodb.github.io/node-mongodb-native/7.1/classes/FindCursor.html#close)
to free up a cursor's resources in both the client application
and the MongoDB Server, as shown in the following example:

```javascript
  await cursor.close();
```

We recommend manually closing any non-exhausted cursors that your application no longer uses
to prevent resource leaks.

> **Tip:** 
>
> Explicit Resource Management
>
> The Node.js driver natively supports explicit resource management for
> `MongoClient`, `ClientSession`, `ChangeStreams`, and cursors. This
> feature is experimental and subject to change. To learn how to use explicit
> resource management, see the [v6.9 Release Notes.](https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/releases/tag/v6.9.0)
